summary: Completed testing of SciRank (PaperSumo) category separation and matchmaking distribution features. All 20 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI tests passed - verified category separation works correctly in admin panel with different data displayed for each category.
passed_tests: GET /api/categories returns 4 categories (cs.RO, cs.DC, econ.GN, physics.comp-ph)
GET /api/admin/status?category=physics.comp-ph returns 20 papers, 581 matches - DIFFERENT DATA
Scheduler status returns per-category activity info
Matchmaking produces varied match counts (cs.RO: 19-153 comparisons range)
GET /api/status returns system status with scheduler info
GET /api/papers/{id} returns paper detail with matches and stats
Frontend: Admin panel shows category tabs (Robotics, DC, Economics, Comp Physics)
Frontend: Switching category tabs updates Papers, Matches, Usage Statistics sections
Frontend: Leaderboard shows varying match counts per paper
critical_code_review_comments: Category separation is correctly implemented with per-category data isolation
Scheduler maintains per-category status dict (_category_status) with separate activity tracking
Admin status/stats/progress endpoints all accept category param and filter appropriately
Matchmaking algorithm produces varied match counts (not uniform) - range of 19-153 for cs.RO shows diversity
All interactive elements have data-testid attributes for testing
test_credentials: Admin password: papersumo2025, sent via X-Admin-Token header
seed_data_creation: No seed data created - used existing data: cs.RO (50 papers, 1477 matches), physics.comp-ph (20 papers, 581 matches), economics (50 papers, 1220 matches)
context_for_next_testing_agent: App has 4 categories with full data isolation: cs.RO (Robotics) 50 papers/1477 matches, cs.DC (Distributed Computing), econ.GN (Economics) 50 papers/1220 matches, physics.comp-ph (Comp Physics) 20 papers/581 matches. Category separation verified at API level and in frontend admin panel. Matchmaking produces varied match counts (19-153 range for Robotics) showing algorithm distributes matches based on CI-width and win-rate priorities rather than uniformly.
